description_long: This object is a long, slightly curved trumpet with a predominantly ivory or bone-colored body and dark brown to black accents at both ends. The bell end is wide and circular, with a pronounced dark rim, while the opposite end tapers to a narrower mouthpiece that is also dark in color. The surface appears smooth and polished, with a subtle sheen, and the trumpet lacks visible valves or complex mechanical parts. The overall design is simple and elegant, suggesting a traditional or ceremonial style rather than a modern orchestral trumpet. The instrument is likely made from a combination of wood, horn, or bone, with some metal or synthetic elements for reinforcement. Its size and construction indicate it is lightweight and portable, suitable for use in outdoor or urban musical performances.
description: A long, slightly curved trumpet with a wide flared bell, ivory body, and dark accents, featuring a smooth, polished surface and no visible valves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, circular bell opening with a dark rim and the gradual tapering of the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gentle curve of the trumpet, the smooth transition from bell to mouthpiece, and the contrasting color bands.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the narrow, dark mouthpiece and the elongated, tapered body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the full length and curvature of the trumpet, with clear visibility of the bell and mouthpiece ends.
attribute_bell_flare: Wide, gradual flare with a pronounced circular opening and dark rim.
attribute_valve_configuration: No visible valves; the body is continuous and unbroken.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: Gently curved, following the overall arc of the instrument.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No visible tuning slide; the body appears to be a single, continuous piece.
